content.full_text:
CalifOrnia
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
July 26, 1990
Nicholas J. Ross
Lindquest & company
certif ied PubIic Accountants
5ooo Executive Parkway, Suite 400
San Ramon, CA 94583
Dear Mr. Ross:
!{e have received your tetter dated June 26 ' 1990, seeking
advice regarding the responsibilities and duties of a broad based
recipient 
- cornmiitee undei the campaign financing and reporting
provisions of the Political Reform Act (rrthe Actrr).r
OUESTIONS
1. 
May a broad based general recipient coumittee make a
contribi.rtion of up to 9i,ooo to a candidate for state office in
another state?
2. If pernissible under the Act, what are the cornnittee's filing
responsibilities as a result of such a contribution?
CONCLUSIONS
1. 
A contribution by a broad based general recipient comroittee
of up to $1,ooo to a Landidate for state office in another state
is permissible under the Act.
2. 
The contribution should be reported as an expenditure on
schedule E of Forn 420.
1 Gorr".rr^.nt code sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
i-gulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations. section
18600, et ECS-,-- A11 references to regrulations are to Tit1e 2,
oivision g oi the california Code of ReguJ-ations.
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-90-439
428 J Street, Suite 800 . P.O. Box 8O7 . sacramento cA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322.5660


Our File No. A-9O-439
Page 2
FACTS
one of your cl-ients, a broad based general recipient
committee located in california, has been solicited for uonetary
contributions by a candidate for the office of governor of the
state of Alaska. Your client wishes to contribute up to 91,OOO to
this canpaign, but has requested your assistance in deternining
the legality of such a contribution and the filing
responsibilj-ties, if any, that such a contribution would create-
ANALYS I S
Atthough contributions by candidates' coromittees to
candidates in other states may be restricted under the Act, there
are no provisions of the Act which prohibit a broad based general
recipient committee fron naking such a contribution.
Sections 85800 et ECIL lJovern the use of campaign funds held
by a broad based general recipient cornmittee. section 85800(a)
piovi.des that chapter 5, Article 8 of the Act applies to any
^cornnittee which gualifies as a conmittee pursuant to subdivision
(a) of Section 82013. The standards of Section 82013(a) encompass
broad based general recipient comrittees.
The general rule applied to the expenditure of campaign funds
is that the expenditures must be related to a political,
Iegislative, oi governmental purpose. (Section 85801). It
.pf".r= that the 
- contribution envisioned by your client would be
related to the purposes specified by Section 85801.
The Cornmission is currently reviewing the applicability of
Section 85800 to each of the use-of-carnpaign-fund sections (Sec-
tions 85800 et seq.). accordingly, the advice given in this 1et-
ter is subject to change and should not be relied upon for future
questions, but only foi the specific situation described in your
letter .
As to the reporting reguirernent, assuming that the
committee files Form 42o, t',te appropriate procedure would be to
report the contribution as an expenditure on Schedule E of that
forn.
Shou1d you have any further question regardingr this natter,
please feel free to contact ne at (915) 322-59oL.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General cofrnsel
It..i.
